Open a terminal on an Arch Linux system with Python and pip installed
Verify the pip version by running `python -m pip --version` to confirm pip is installed and check its version
Navigate to the root directory of the TensorFlow Object Detection API repository where the setup.py file is located
Attempt to run the following command to install the package using the deprecated resolver feature: `python -m pip install --use-feature=2020-resolver .`
Observe the output error message from pip indicating `option --use-feature: invalid choice: '2020-resolver' (choose from 'fast-deps', 'truststore','no-binary-enable-wheel-cache')`
Capture and store the full stderr and stdout output of the command for validation
Assert that the error message explicitly contains the substring `invalid choice: '2020-resolver'`
Assert that the pip usage instructions are printed as part of the output, confirming pip rejected the option
Optionally, check the pip version again to correlate the unsupported feature with the pip version installed
Document the failure reproducing the installation command exactly as given in the TensorFlow Models GitHub instructions under 'Python Package Installation'
